D9 BiologyL6 CreateReference

Photoswitchable FPs — Toggle Fluorescence with Light

PA-GFP and Dronpa toggle between dark and bright states with specific wavelengths — used for super-resolution PALM and pulse-chase live-cell imaging

PA-GFP (photo-activatable): dark by default; 405 nm pulse cis→trans isomerises the chromophore and unleashes bright 488 nm-excitable green. Dronpa: switches both ways (488 nm dims, 405 nm restores). PALM single-molecule localisation needs this on/off switching to reconstruct super-resolution images below the diffraction limit.

Toggle laser to activate or quench.
